tossups.question: This author ended a poem by referencing Auden with the line, “Bring me the wine-cellar key. Let the less-loving one be me.” This poet wrote, “we stay trapped in time, / queuing for death,” in a poem which begins, “Thought of by you all day, I think of you.” A poem by this author which ends with the speaker stating “Long time since I walked / down Lonely Street / towards Heartbreak Hotel” is from a collection also containing a poem whose speaker recounts how her husband “held me upon that (*) next best bed.” Chiwetel Ejiofor starred in this author’s adaptation of Everyman. This author of Rapture included “Elvis’s Twin Sister” and “Anne Hathaway” in a collection told from the perspectives of female counterparts of famous men. In 2019, this poet was succeeded in one honorary position by Simon Armitage. For 10 points, name this poet of The World’s Wife, the first female British poet laureate.
tossups.question_sanitized: This author ended a poem by referencing Auden with the line, "Bring me the wine-cellar key. Let the less-loving one be me." This poet wrote, "we stay trapped in time, / queuing for death," in a poem which begins, "Thought of by you all day, I think of you." A poem by this author which ends with the speaker stating "Long time since I walked / down Lonely Street / towards Heartbreak Hotel" is from a collection also containing a poem whose speaker recounts how her husband "held me upon that (*) next best bed." Chiwetel Ejiofor starred in this author's adaptation of Everyman. This author of Rapture included "Elvis's Twin Sister" and "Anne Hathaway" in a collection told from the perspectives of female counterparts of famous men. In 2019, this poet was succeeded in one honorary position by Simon Armitage. For 10 points, name this poet of The World's Wife, the first female British poet laureate.
tossups.answer: Carol Ann Duffy (The first poem is “Mrs. Beast.”)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Carol Ann Duffy (The first poem is "Mrs. Beast.")

tossups.question: This poet wrote a response to Robert Burns’s “Mary Morison” in which the speaker describes “hours and wishes and trysts / less than the shadows of clouds on grass” and says love is “itself a metaphor, rose red.” This poet of “Sung” created a speaker who defends gifting their lover an onion by stating “Its platinum loops shrink to a wedding ring / if you like” in “Valentine.” This author wrote “If poetry could truly tell it backwards, / then it would” in a poem that inverts (*) Wilfred Owen’s “Dulce et Decorum Est.” This author eulogized two of the longest-lived veterans of World War One in the poem “Last Post.” The statement “It does not look like me” ends this author’s poem written from the perspective of an artist’s model. This author of “Standing Female Nude” wrote the collections Rapture and The World’s Wife. For 10 points, what lesbian Scottish author was the British Poet Laureate until May 2019?
tossups.question_sanitized: This poet wrote a response to Robert Burns's "Mary Morison" in which the speaker describes "hours and wishes and trysts / less than the shadows of clouds on grass" and says love is "itself a metaphor, rose red." This poet of "Sung" created a speaker who defends gifting their lover an onion by stating "Its platinum loops shrink to a wedding ring / if you like" in "Valentine." This author wrote "If poetry could truly tell it backwards, / then it would" in a poem that inverts (*) Wilfred Owen's "Dulce et Decorum Est." This author eulogized two of the longest-lived veterans of World War One in the poem "Last Post." The statement "It does not look like me" ends this author's poem written from the perspective of an artist's model. This author of "Standing Female Nude" wrote the collections Rapture and The World's Wife. For 10 points, what lesbian Scottish author was the British Poet Laureate until May 2019?

bonuses.leadin: In a Warsan Shire poem set at a center for this process, the speaker calls themselves “bloated with language I can’t afford to forget” and has a mouth that “becomes a sink full of blood.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In a Warsan Shire poem set at a center for this process, the speaker calls themselves "bloated with language I can't afford to forget" and has a mouth that "becomes a sink full of blood." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this process. While living in England, Obinze is forced to undergo this process on the day of his wedding to Cleotilde in Chimamanda Adichie’s novel Americanah.

The speaker wonders how to tell her daughter they’re being deported in “Whilst Leila Sleeps,” a poem by a poet from this country. That poet, Jackie Kay, dated a poet from this country who wrote Standing Female Nude and The World’s Wife.

A Patience Agbabi poem reimagines this character as a Nigerian immigrant named “Mrs Alice Ebi Bafa.” A Zadie Smith play set in 18th-century Jamaica retells this Chaucer character’s tale about what women desire most.

the Wife of Bath [prompt on “The Wife of Bafa” or The Wife of Willesden]

bonuses.leadin: In a poem by this author the speaker tells the reader she “made quite sure” a wolf spotted her for the sake of “poetry.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In a poem by this author the speaker tells the reader she "made quite sure" a wolf spotted her for the sake of "poetry." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this poet whose speaker takes an axe to the wolf in “Little Red-Cap.” She included a poem in the same collection about Shakespeare’s wife who holds “him in the casket of my widow’s head.”
Carol Ann Duffy was the first woman to occupy this position. In 2019, Simon Armitage succeeded her in this position.

Aside from writing poetry, Armitage also translated a middle English poem in which this character, not yet a member of King Arthur’s Roundtable, returns to finish a beheading game with the Green Knight started at Christmas.

bonuses.leadin: A poem by this author was censored in schools in 2008 on the basis of its references to knife crime. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A poem by this author was censored in schools in 2008 on the basis of its references to knife crime. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of the collection Standing Female Nude. This poet’s poem “Education for Leisure” begins “Today I am going to kill something. Anything.” and sees its speaker kill a fly and a goldfish.
Carol Ann Duffy was the first woman to hold this literary position, which she held from 2009 to 2019. Holders of this position, currently held by Simon Armitage, write verse for national occasions.
Duffy’s collection Standing Female Nude contains this other poem, also noted for the use of the voice of an outsider. This poem begins “I have turned the newspaper boy into a diver for pearls.”.
parts_sanitized: Name this author of the collection Standing Female Nude. This poet's poem "Education for Leisure" begins "Today I am going to kill something. Anything." and sees its speaker kill a fly and a goldfish.
Carol Ann Duffy was the first woman to hold this literary position, which she held from 2009 to 2019. Holders of this position, currently held by Simon Armitage, write verse for national occasions.
Duffy's collection Standing Female Nude contains this other poem, also noted for the use of the voice of an outsider. This poem begins "I have turned the newspaper boy into a diver for pearls.".
answers_sanitized: Poet Laureate of the United Kingdom

bonuses.leadin: The speaker asks if it was “So very hard to die” in a poem in which this figure is asked to stand “Flame-capped, and shout for me,” titled for this figure “in the Trench.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The speaker asks if it was "So very hard to die" in a poem in which this figure is asked to stand "Flame-capped, and shout for me," titled for this figure "in the Trench." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this mythological figure whose mother “of the shining breasts / Cried out in dismay” in a W. H. Auden poem about his shield. This man kills Hector in The Iliad.

The title man “survives among hills” while “the dust of Achilles and Cuchulain itches” in this author’s poem “Dick Straightup.” This poet described a “hot stink of fox” in “The Thought-Fox,” part of the collection The Hawk in the Rain.

This poet mythologized David Beckham as Achilles in a poem about Beckham’s Achilles tendon injury. Famous men’s female counterparts narrate several dramatic monologues in this poet’s collection The World’s Wife.
parts_sanitized: Name this mythological figure whose mother "of the shining breasts / Cried out in dismay" in a W. H. Auden poem about his shield. This man kills Hector in The Iliad.

The title man "survives among hills" while "the dust of Achilles and Cuchulain itches" in this author's poem "Dick Straightup." This poet described a "hot stink of fox" in "The Thought-Fox," part of the collection The Hawk in the Rain.

This poet mythologized David Beckham as Achilles in a poem about Beckham's Achilles tendon injury. Famous men's female counterparts narrate several dramatic monologues in this poet's collection The World's Wife.
answers: Achilles [accept “Achilles in the Trench” or “The Shield of Achilles”]
answers_sanitized: Achilles [accept "Achilles in the Trench" or "The Shield of Achilles"]

bonuses.leadin: In one poem, the speaker likens this person’s words to “shooting stars which fell to Earth as kisses” after calling an heirloom “a spinning world of forests, castles, torchlight, [and] clifftops.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one poem, the speaker likens this person's words to "shooting stars which fell to Earth as kisses" after calling an heirloom "a spinning world of forests, castles, torchlight, [and] clifftops." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this person who, in a John Milton poem, is addressed as a “son of Memory” that lies under a “star-ypointing pyramid.”

In “Anne Hathaway,” Carol Ann Duffy likens Hathaway and Shakespeare’s marriage bed to “seas where he would dive for pearls,” potentially alluding to a song which this character sings. This spirit sings “Full Fathom Five.”
In response to the censorship of one of her poems, Duffy wrote “Mrs. Schofield’s GCSE,” in which she references this Shakespeare play about “star-cross’d lovers” with a line about “Tybalt’s death.”
parts_sanitized: Name this person who, in a John Milton poem, is addressed as a "son of Memory" that lies under a "star-ypointing pyramid."

In "Anne Hathaway," Carol Ann Duffy likens Hathaway and Shakespeare's marriage bed to "seas where he would dive for pearls," potentially alluding to a song which this character sings. This spirit sings "Full Fathom Five."
In response to the censorship of one of her poems, Duffy wrote "Mrs. Schofield's GCSE," in which she references this Shakespeare play about "star-cross'd lovers" with a line about "Tybalt's death."
answers: William Shakespeare [or “On Shakespeare, 1630”] (The poems are “Anne Hathaway” by Carol Ann Duffy, and “On Shakespeare, 1630” by John Milton.)
answers_sanitized: William Shakespeare [or "On Shakespeare, 1630"] (The poems are "Anne Hathaway" by Carol Ann Duffy, and "On Shakespeare, 1630" by John Milton.)

bonuses.leadin: In a poem by this author, “poetry” is given as the reason why a character lets a wolf buy her a drink and then kills him with an ax 10 years later. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of “Little Red-Cap.” This author collected poems about female counterparts to famous male figures such as Charles Darwin and Pontius Pilate in The World’s Wife.

In a poem from The World’s Wife, this man’s widow reflects on being bequeathed his “second best bed.” That poem, “Anne Hathaway,” is set after this author is buried in his birthplace of Stratford-upon-Avon.

Description acceptable. In a Duffy poem, this person “ages one year in an hour” waiting to celebrate her “Christmas Birthday.” A poem addressing this person as a “dear, dear Friend” notes that “five years have past” since a visit to the title place.
answers: Dorothy Wordsworth [accept descriptions involving William Wordsworth’s sister; prompt on Wordsworth] (The first poem is “Dorothy Wordsworth’s Christmas Birthday.” The second poem is “Tintern Abbey” by William Wordsworth.)
answers_sanitized: Dorothy Wordsworth [accept descriptions involving William Wordsworth's sister; prompt on Wordsworth] (The first poem is "Dorothy Wordsworth's Christmas Birthday." The second poem is "Tintern Abbey" by William Wordsworth.)

bonuses.leadin: A poetry performance collective named for this city's "Scene" became sufficiently popular to open for Led Zeppelin on a 1969 tour. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city. The Sound of this U.K. city's main river names an influential 1960s collection compiling poems by Roger McGough, Adrian Henri, and Brian Patten.

The Mersey Sound is an entry in this publisher's "Modern Poets" series. Paperbacks were partly popularized by the "Modern Classics" series released by this British publisher, which is named for a bird.

Adrian Henri, one of the poets featured in The Mersey Sound, later had a decade-long relationship with this Scottish poet of The World's Wife. In 2009, this poet became the first woman and first lesbian to be selected as British Poet Laureate.
answers: Penguin Books [or Penguin Modern Poets; or Penguin Modern Classics]
answers_sanitized: Liverpool [accept The Liverpool Scene or Liverpool poets]
Penguin Books [or Penguin Modern Poets; or Penguin Modern Classics]

bonuses.leadin: Kae Tempest's poetry collection Hold Your Own explores this figure's life through sections such as “Manhood,” “Womanhood,” and “Blind Profit” and includes an epigraph from Oedipus Rex. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this figure from Greek mythology. A poem from the view of this character's fictional wife describes how this figure “went out for his walk a man / and came home female.”

An earlier poetry collection by Tempest, Brand New Ancients, won the Ted Hughes Award, which was funded by this poet. This poet described “Mrs. Tiresias” and other reimagined females in her collection The World's Wife.
Both Ted Hughes and Carol Ann Duffy held this position in the United Kingdom, which is currently held by Simon Armitage.
answers: Tiresias [do not accept or prompt on “Mrs. Tiresias”]

Poet Laureate of the United Kingdom

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer the following about British literature inspired by Brexit.
parts: A Scottish author with this surname wrote the "first serious post Brexit novel," Autumn, about the relationship between a 101-year-old retired songwriter and a young lecturer. Another author with this surname wrote the postcolonial novel White Teeth.

The Cockroach, a recent political satire by this British author, follows a man who wakes up as Prime Minister who must get Britain to leave the EU. This author wrote about the double murder of Clive and Vernon in his novel Amsterdam.

This author, who was succeeded in May 2019 by Simon Armitage as Poet Laureate, anthropomorphized the UK in her post Brexit poem "Campaign." Three female Magi appear in her poem "Queen Herod," which she included in her collection The World's Wife.
answers_sanitized: Smith [accept Ali Smith; or Zadie Smith; or Sadie Smith]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, answer the following about British literature inspired by Brexit.
parts_sanitized: A Scottish author with this surname wrote the "first serious post Brexit novel," Autumn, about the relationship between a 101-year-old retired songwriter and a young lecturer. Another author with this surname wrote the postcolonial novel White Teeth.

The Cockroach, a recent political satire by this British author, follows a man who wakes up as Prime Minister who must get Britain to leave the EU. This author wrote about the double murder of Clive and Vernon in his novel Amsterdam.

This author, who was succeeded in May 2019 by Simon Armitage as Poet Laureate, anthropomorphized the UK in her post Brexit poem "Campaign." Three female Magi appear in her poem "Queen Herod," which she included in her collection The World's Wife.

bonuses.leadin: Thom Yorke based the Radiohead song “Subterranean Homesick Alien” on a school assignment to imitate the style of a poetry movement named for this sort of creature. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this sort of figure, the namesake of a school of poetry led by Craig Raine that sought to defamiliarize ordinary life. These creatures invade Earth in H. G. Wells’s The War of the Worlds.

Raine’s “A Martian Sends a Postcard Home” calls these objects “caxtons” after the man who first made them in England. Robert Herrick listed items like “blossoms, birds, and bowers (“BAO-ers”)” in an “Argument” of one of these things.
This poet of “Warming Her Pearls” is a member of the “New Generation” that succeeded the Martian school. The speaker recalls turning a “buzzing bee” to a “dull grey pebble” in this poet’s “Medusa.”
answers: Martians [or aliens from Mars; accept Martian poetry or Martian school of poetry; prompt on aliens or extraterrestrials or ETs by asking “from where?”; prompt on tripods by asking “piloted by creatures from where?”]
books [accept equivalents including poetry collections and printed books; or incunabula; accept “The Argument of His Book”]

packet.name: A - Columbia A and Texas A

bonuses.leadin: A novel by this author interweaves the narrative of the recently-bereaved British teenager George and the narrative of the Renaissance fresco painter Francesco del Cossa. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Scottish author whose Booker Prize-nominated novels include How to be Both and a 2016 novel about centenarian songwriter Daniel Gluck.

That latter novel by Smith, which has been called the first post-Brexit novel, has this title. This season is also the subject of a Keats ode, which describes it as the “season of mists and mellow fruitfulness, close bosom-friend of the maturing sun.”

The Brexit vote also inspired the play My Country: A Work in Progress by this Scottish poet and playwright. Her poetry collections include The World’s Wife, and she is the current British Poet Laureate.
answers_sanitized: Autumn [do not accept or prompt on "Fall"]

bonuses.leadin: According to this collection, Pontius Pilate’s fingernails were “like shells from Galilee”, and a campanologist “fucked [Mrs Quasimodo] underneath the gaping, stricken bells”. For 10 points each:
parts: Women of literature and history share their stories in this collection of poems, which opens with Little Red Cap.
The World’s Wife was written by this feminist Poet Laureate, who also won the T.S. Eliot Prize for her anthology, Rapture.
A nun thinks of her convent as “a land of grace” in this poem in The World’s Wife. Its speaker says it has been a long time since she “walked down Lonely Street / towards Heartbreak Hotel”.

bonuses.leadin: “Ode on the Death of the Duke of Wellington” and “Charge of the Light Brigade” were both written by a holder of this office. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this honorary literary position in the United Kingdom, holders of which are expected to write poetry for important national events. Alfred Tennyson had this role for 42 years, and Ted Hughes had this role for 14 years.
This first official poet laureate under Charles II received an annual “butt of Canary wine” along with his monetary salary. His works include Annus Mirabilis and the satirical Mac Flecknoe.

This current British poet laureate is the first woman, Scot, or openly LGBT person to hold the position. She wrote from the perspective of an artist’s model in “Standing Female Nude,” which also titles her first collection.
answers_sanitized: Poet Laureate of the United Kingdom

This current British poet laureate is the first woman, Scot, or openly LGBT person to hold the position. She wrote from the perspective of an artist's model in "Standing Female Nude," which also titles her first collection.
